fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 77 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False (i.e., `not clamp`) instead of when `clamp` is True. The `if not clamp:` should be `if clamp:`.

spec.expr: clamp == True or (not clamp and True)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the branch body (clamping logic) should execute; the condition `not clamp` is inverted so clamping happens when clamp=False instead.
The then-branch of `if not clamp` executes only when clamp is False, but the clamping logic should run when clamp is True — this branch is entered under the wrong condition.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: After the clamping block, if clamp is True then y__4 should be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the inverted condition causes y__4 to be unclamped when clamp=True.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Before y__4 is used in the final max/min expression, if clamp=True it must already be restricted to the reference range.
